use cases

they're going to stop balance sheet
runoff in the first half of 2025 uh is
for a couple of reasons one prior to the
debt limit um uh issue uh debt limit
being uh breached uh you know prior to
the TGA spin down which is currently
artificially inflating uh Bank Reserves
we were kind of approaching levels uh uh
from A Bank Reserves perspective when
you look at it as a ratio of let's say
GDP or Commercial Bank assets or
Commercial Bank liabilities Commercial
Bank deposits all those ratios were
starting to get to levels that were
consistent with uh the stress that we
saw on the banking system during the
regional banking crisis and so they're
kind of getting close to that signal
when they were going to get the
transition from an abundant reserves
regime to an ample reserves regime
already now what's delaying that now is
to draw down the TGA which is inflating
Bank Reserves and so the FED has to be
concerned that when the debt limit does
get lifted and we have this massive
spike in the TGA when they start to
replenish it there we may crash through
uh those levels in terms of reserves
when on on on the way to the downside
and they're going to in my opinion going
to want to try to avoid that from a
financial stability perspective because
if they do it's going to have a
significant ific impact on on the repo
market and and obviously we know the FED
is hyper sensitive to that so it's our
view that they're probably going to stop
balance sheet runoff uh either today uh
in May or in June I don't know which but
in our opinion they got to do it before
uh the Deb limit is lifted and the TJ
starts to spike again so uh going back
to the question is this incremental
liquidity enough to give Market support
um yeah yeah they it will give Market

tightening or EAS policy which is
effectively the same thing from the

provide support to asset markets now the

again goes back to the you know who

could get this you know this this
situation where markets are you know
kind of down 10 up Seven 8 n down
another 10 up six seven you know and
kind of this whipsaw um dynamic because
again what ultimately is happening here
is there's this kind of pushpull between
the right tail and the left tail I've
been talking on this program and
obviously in our in our publish research
to our clients about how wide the
distribution of probable economic
outcomes is it's not all bearish you
know we we do believe that us growth is
likely to slow over the medium term
faster than what consensus expects and
that is not even close to being priced
in those Dynamics when you look at
valuations uh estimates for S&P types
company sales and earnings or GDP
estimates none of that stuff is budged
materially enough to suggest that we're
pricing in a real true growth scare um
so that in my opinion I still think
there's left tail risk associated with
having to price that in um there's
obviously left tail risk associated with
having to replenish the TGA as well but
there's also right to risk associated
with you know potential tariff the the
boogeyman that is terrorist maybe gets
less at the margins relative to current
expectations uh you obviously have um
what has been you know positive um uh
fiscal ort respective of tax cuts and
and deregulation you know the kind of
the positive aspects of the Trump agenda
uh there's some negative aspects of the
Trump agenda that are contributing to
the faster than expected growth slowdown
and and and faster than expected
inflation that we're currently expecting
out of our grid model over the medium
term so trying to draw a uniform linear
straight line to the market destination
from here is a Fool's errand what you
should do as an investor is expect
markets to go down a lot have these long
multi-week you know counter Trend
bounces uh that's let me tell you what
our expectation is we expect markets to
eventually go down a lot we do also
expect because of the sheer amount of
girth associated with the the the area
under the the right side of the
distribution of probable economic
outcomes you know the right toe risk is
there's a lot of right toe risk still
there not the least of which is monetary
policy um so you you're probably going
to have these multi-week periods where
Market are rallying these multi-week
periods where markets are declining and
ultimately we still think the
probability of recognizing a crash in
2025 is much higher than the consensus
is allowed to believe right now because
they're not doing the kind of research
that we're doing to understand the risk
of a global refinancing air pocket uh
starting in the second half of this year
or it could be starting as soon as now
uh you know I don't want to say I don't
I'm not smart enough to tell you exactly
when it starts but I am smart enough to
tell you that the risk is uh much more
elevated than the average person
realizes so that is still a big risk
this year I think that will be
exacerbated by a sharper than expected
slowdown in US growth a up upside
surprises in US inflation kind of
starting in the second quarter so
there's a lot of left tail risk that has
not been priced in there's a lot of
right tail risk that was priced in to
some degree if you think about the
hopium and Trump bump that we got after
the election so in my opinion I think on
net it still favors a bare Market over a
medium term however a we could be wrong
about all that uh and B that doesn't
mean we have to go into bare Market
territory in a linear straight line
markets don't go anywhere in a linear
straight line they're going to have cter
ralles and so you Mr and Mrs investor
who been listening to me Rand about this
for 10 minutes you need to make up your
mind what is your goal is your goal to
be a hedge fund manager or Trader and
trade every like two 300 point in the
Spy or you know 10,000 Point uh dollar
move in Bitcoin or do you want to get
the Thousand Point move in the Spy r or
the $10,000 move in Bitcoin right right
like to me that to me is more important
uh if your goal is to retire on time and
comfortably and that's what our research
is designed to do is to help retail
investors retire on time and comfortably
and to help Wall Street portfolio
managers and cios make fat bonuses and
so we have elements of our research that
support the trading activities of of the
the LOD of investors the the
institutional investors but if you're a
retail investor listening to me talk if
your goal is to try to trade every
little wiggle in the stock market every
the little way go in the crypto Market
42 macro is not the research provider
for you 42 macro is very good and making
sure you capture the bulk of the uptrend
in Bull markets and you know minimizing
downside risk in bare markets and making
sure that you actually book those gains
when you're at or near the top of bull
markets that's what we do exceptionally
well um so hopefully you know my
long-winded response to this question
can sort of um elucidate some of the the
the elements that we are uh deliberating
at the current juncture to kind of help
clients understand where this is likely
all headed uh but you also need to
realize that we're not going to head
anywhere in financial Market Mark
especially uh in a bare Market U because
bare markets have more aggressive
bounces than bull markets have
Corrections especially in a bare Market
you're not going to uh have a linear
straight line to the destination so just
be aware of that understand what your
goals and objectives are as an investor
and stay true to those goals and
objectives don't get S if you're a
long-term investor trying to retire on
time and comfortably don't get sucked
into the top of a of a multi- reek rally
in a bare Market uh in so much that uh
if your goal uh uh is to retire on time
and comfortably and I would said this to
you last year don't start shorting the
market too soon because you think you're
smarter than the market and and you're
want to fight you know all the flows
associated with the Raging bow market so
our message is consistent no matter if
we're in a bull market or bare Market
it's maximize upside capture when you're
in the bull market minimize downside
capture when you're in the bare market
right now we think we are uh in the you
know early Innings of a bare Market um
